page: Microsoft Azure FX32ms_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8573C x86_64 processor with 16 physical cores and 32 dedicated vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions) for deep learning operations. The server is configured with 672.0 GB of system memory, yielding 21.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory and processing capabilities. The server achieves top-tier performance (top 10%) in Geekbench single-core, image processing, and text processing tests. Large-block memory bandwidth read and write operations rank in the top 25%, reflecting efficient data throughput for memory-bound tasks. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and static web serving throughput also place in the top 25%. However, while prompt processing for medium and large language models performs in the average to strong range, text generation for large 70B models falls into the bottom 25%, highlighting a performance tradeoff for large-scale generative AI workloads without dedicated GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX32ms_v2 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it a resource-dense option for workloads requiring massive RAM footprints without the overhead of excessive CPU cores. The absence of local storage means storage costs are decoupled, requiring external premium storage attachments. This configuration is suitable for memory-intensive applications, large relational databases, in-memory caching systems, and enterprise applications requiring high single-core CPU speeds alongside substantial memory capacity.
description: Microsoft Azure FX32ms_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine featuring an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and a high clock speed of 4.2 GHz. It is equipped with 672.0 GB of RAM, providing a substantial 21.0 GB of memory per core, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Built on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server delivers top-tier single-core, image processing, and text processing performance in Geekbench benchmarks, alongside strong large-block memory bandwidth. While it exhibits average performance in database operations and weak text generation speeds for large 70B language models, its high memory density and advanced CPU instructions like Intel AMX make it highly suitable for memory-bound enterprise workloads, in-memory databases, and heavy data processing tasks that do not require local NVMe storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX32ms_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 672 GB of RAM, and a 4.2 GHz clock speed, optimized for memory-bound enterprise workloads.

tagline: A high-frequency, memory-dense virtual machine featuring 672 GB of RAM and dedicated Intel Xeon processors for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs at 4.2 GHz
672.0 GB of system memory providing 21.0 GB per physical core
Top-tier Geekbench single-core and text processing performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for memory-intensive databases and enterprise applications
